definitions: To deal with a problem only when it becomes necessary, rather than worrying about it in advance.
To postpone worrying about a potential future problem until it actually arises.
examples: I'm not sure how we'll fund the project, but we'll cross that bridge when we come to it.
Don't worry about what might happen next year; we'll cross that bridge when we come to it.
We don't have a plan for if the power goes out, but we'll cross that bridge when we come to it.
lemma: cross that bridge when you come to it
source: cross that bridge when you come to it
